topic.title: Assignment 7 — Meet Nina. She's Going to Help You. And Push Back on Everything.
topic.text:
Hi everyone,

Two things before you start Assignment 7.

First — a tool.

You may have noticed that Professor Nik built something in class on Saturday. That something is Nina — an AI Creative Director with 35 years of real brand strategy and creative direction expertise, available 24/7, in any language, trained to help you build exactly what Assignment 7 asks for.

Nina will walk you through your creative brief, your visual identity, your UVP, your logo concept directions, your wireframe strategy, and your platform recommendation — interactively, the way a Creative Director works with a junior in a studio.

You can access Nina here: https://chatgpt.com/g/g-69b5a5d37a4c819197853cc8e2b8e445-nina

You will need a ChatGPT account to use Nina. Free accounts work — just sign up at chatgpt.com if you don't already have one.

Second — I'm doing the assignment with you.

I started Sunday morning and finished by midday. I've attached my complete Assignment 7 submission to this announcement — creative brief, visual identity, color system, typography, logo directions, wireframe strategy, and platform recommendation — all built using Nina.

I'm sharing it for one reason: to show you what the process looks like when it's done properly. Not a filled-in template. Not a first draft. The result of being interrogated, corrected, and made to go deeper than the comfortable answer.

This is the bar. Show me something that gets over it.

A few things worth knowing before you start:

Nina pushes back. On every decision. With reasoning. That's not the tool being difficult — that's the tool doing its job. A good Creative Director won't tell you what you want to hear. As I've always said: a good Creative Director will tell you to go to hell — but you look forward to the trip.

The intake questions are the assignment. Everything Nina produces is only as good as the answers you give it. Vague answers produce vague output. Specific, honest answers produce something that might actually surprise you.

You must include one moment of disagreement. Somewhere in your submission, show me a place where you pushed back on Nina's recommendation, argued for something different, and made your own call. Describe what Nina suggested, why you changed it, and why your decision was right. This is the requirement that proves you did the thinking — not just the typing.

Nina does the assignment with you, not for you. The conversation is the work. The PDF is the documentation of that conversation turned into a deliverable.

What Nina helps you build:

  • Creative brief with UVP — three iterations with reasoning (copy UVP from assignment 6)
  • Brand archetype with shadow risk
  • Visual identity: color system with hex codes, contrast checking, typography
  • Logo concept directions with AI generation prompts for Canva AI and Adobe Firefly
  • Website wireframes — five pages with user flow narrative
  • Platform comparison and recommendation
  • Brand style guide foundation

Tools you will need:

Color: Go to Coolors (coolors.co) first. Develop your color palette there — explore, lock colors you love, build your system. Once you have your palette, copy the hex codes and bring them into Nina. Nina will then name them, rationale them, and build your contrast matrix from the exact values you chose.

Contrast checking: Run every color combination through WebAIM Contrast Checker (webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker) before it goes in your submission. Color contrast is the mistake most non-designers make. Top quartile submissions catch it. Bottom quartile submissions don't know it's a problem.

Wireframes: Use the Figma free wireframing kit (figma.com/templates/wireframe-kits/) to build your five-page website structure. Use your Figma board for your full visual direction — moodboard, color swatches, logo iterations, and wireframes all in one place. Figma is free for students at figma.com/education.

I still need to finish my Figma board with the visual assets — logo iterations, moodboard, and color system page. So we're in the same place. You have until Friday at midnight. So do I.

 

Thanks, Profs. Nina & Nik
